status: COMPLETE — both HITL-ruled terminal codex P2s closed; all gates green

commits.note: GREEN was amended once to fold in an error-message accuracy refinement (engine-only string, asserted by no test); the TS fresh-clone gate was re-run at the final SHA after the amend.

F1_temporal_round_trip.class: TC-47 — calendar-impossible DAY rollover residue behind the TC-44 strftime-leniency class
F1_temporal_round_trip.bug: strftime('%s', ?) ROLLS OVER a shape-valid but impossible DAY instead of returning NULL: 2025-02-30T00:00:00Z -> epoch for 2025-03-02; 2025-04-31T00:00:00Z -> 2025-05-01. TC-44's is_iso8601_shape gate checks FORMAT not calendar validity, so Feb-30 passed the gate and stored a DIFFERENT instant than the provider supplied — bypassing the ratified hard-reject contract. (Impossible MONTHS like 2025-13-01 and impossible TIMES like 25:00:00 / :60 / :61 already NULL out; only impossible DAYS roll over — the sole residue.)
F1_temporal_round_trip.fix_location: src/rust/crates/fathomdb-engine/src/lib.rs — iso8601_to_epoch_seconds (the shared parse used by normalize_extractor_timestamp for both t_valid and t_invalid at the fathomdb.extract.v1 write boundary).
F1_temporal_round_trip.round_trip_sql: SELECT CAST(strftime('%s', ?1) AS INTEGER) WHERE strftime('%Y-%m-%d', substr(?1, 1, 10)) IS substr(?1, 1, 10)
F1_temporal_round_trip.sql_rationale: The WHERE clause is the round-trip: the literal calendar DATE component of the input (substr(?1,1,10) = the YYYY-MM-DD the shape gate guarantees present) must survive SQLite's own calendar math UNCHANGED. On a DAY rollover, strftime('%Y-%m-%d', substr) differs from the literal substring, the WHERE yields zero rows, query_row -> QueryReturnedNoRows -> .ok().flatten() -> None, and the EXISTING hard-reject Err path in normalize_extractor_timestamp fires. The shape gate is KEPT for its early clear message; the round-trip is the completeness backstop behind it (defence in depth).
F1_temporal_round_trip.why_date_component_not_raw_or_utc_render: A raw-string comparison or a strftime(...,'unixepoch') UTC re-render would FALSE-REJECT valid equivalent forms, because the UTC render shifts the wall clock for a non-UTC offset and its date can differ from the input's literal date. Comparing ONLY the literal DATE field (substr 1..10) is timezone-INVARIANT — the offset never alters the input's own YYYY-MM-DD text — while still catching every DAY rollover, because the rollover happens in the calendar math BEFORE the offset is applied. This is the tz trap the mandate warned about, engineered out.
F1_temporal_round_trip.completeness_superset: The round-trip is a SUPERSET of the shape gate for the Julian case: substr('2451545.0',1,10) = '2451545.0' renders via strftime('%Y-%m-%d',...) to '2000-01-01', which != '2451545.0' -> rejected. So the TC-44 Julian string is rejected by the round-trip independently of the shape gate.
F1_temporal_round_trip.no_date_crate: CONFIRMED — pure SQL. No chrono/time (or any date crate) added; the workspace still has none. That option was OFF the table per mandate.
F1_temporal_round_trip.null_reject_path_untouched: CONFIRMED — the None/JSON-null/absent -> Ok(None) arm of normalize_extractor_timestamp (NULL means 'still valid') is byte-unchanged. The fix only widened the set of String inputs that iso8601_to_epoch_seconds returns None for (rolled-over DAYS), which routes into the EXISTING hard-reject Err path — never into the NULL/Ok(None) path. No resurrection-via-NULL surface was opened.
F1_temporal_round_trip.surviving_class_escalation: NONE. No malformed-but-well-shaped temporal value survives the round-trip: impossible MONTHS and impossible TIMES already NULL out via strftime; impossible DAYS are caught by the DATE round-trip; the Julian string is caught by both the shape gate and the round-trip. No fix-N past the round-trip was added; no escalation to 'add a date library' was triggered.

positive_forms_accepted_not_false_reject.proof: All five ACCEPTED and stored at the CORRECT epoch, asserted at the raw at-rest edge table (t_valid, typeof=integer) in tc47_timestamp_round_trip.rs. The +05:00 case (1742445000) is the load-bearing tz-trap control: its epoch differs from the +00:00/Z case (1742463000) by exactly 5h, proving the offset is honoured and not false-rejected.

F2_ts_projection_ffi_guard.class: AC-068 / TC-47 — TS configureProjections skipped the JS-side FFI surrogate guard
F2_ts_projection_ffi_guard.bug: src/ts/src/index.ts configureProjections did NOT call the FFI string guard before the native call. napi-rs silently replaces a lone UTF-16 surrogate with U+FFFD BEFORE the Rust-side guard runs, so a JS caller passing a name/role/ftsTokenizer/vectorEmbedder/drop entry with a lone surrogate got U+FFFD persisted instead of WriteValidationError. (A NUL survives the napi UTF-8 path as a real byte and was already caught Rust-side — which is why the pre-existing NUL tests passed but the surrogate leaked.)
F2_ts_projection_ffi_guard.fix_location: src/ts/src/index.ts — configureProjections now calls validateFfiTree(specs) and validateFfiTree(drop) BEFORE this.#native.configureProjections(...). validateFfiTree recursively validates every string in the tree: projection name, each role, ftsTokenizer, vectorEmbedder, and each drop entry. Mirrors the existing write() call site (validateFfiTree(batch)) and the AC-068b surrogate handling in validation.ts.
